mnemonics: CLT is a TLC for your data: Tender Loving Central tendency. It says that no matter the original shape of the data's distribution, the distribution of the sample means will tend to be Normal as the sample size grows.
keyPoints: The sampling distribution is the probability distribution of a sample statistic (like the mean) calculated from many random samples of the same size from the same population.

The Central Limit Theorem (CLT) states that the sampling distribution of the sample mean approximates a normal distribution as the sample size gets larger, regardless of the population's distribution.
The standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the mean is called the standard error, calculated as SE = σ/√n, and it measures the variability of the sample mean around the population mean.
commonMistakes: Confusing the standard deviation of the population (σ) with the standard error of the mean (σ/√n). The standard error is always smaller.
Assuming the sampling distribution is normal for small sample sizes (n < 30) when the population distribution is not normal. The CLT requires a sufficiently large n.
Thinking the sampling distribution is the same as the distribution of a single sample. The sampling distribution is a theoretical distribution of a statistic across many samples.
